title: chamaesyce deltoidea subsp. adhaerens
intro: Chamaesyce deltoidea subsp. adhaerens, also known as Spreading Spurge or Spurge Weed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to North America. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, wounds, and inflammation. The health benefits of Chamaesyce deltoidea subsp. adhaerens include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection, promoting wound healing and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made from the leaves, roots, and stems of the plant.
